# Sahaf — Complete Documentation

Local PDF & EPUB to Markdown converter with automatic digital/scanned detection, OCR support, smart splitting, and page-range selection. Converts books to clean, self-contained Markdown files with embedded images using Marker (95.67% accuracy) and Surya OCR (90+ languages). No cloud APIs — runs entirely on your hardware.

## Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/arikusi/sahaf.git
cd sahaf
pip install -e .
```

Marker models (~2-3GB) are downloaded automatically on first conversion. Requires Python 3.10+, 4-6GB RAM.

## Run

```bash
sahaf
```

Or manually: `uvicorn backend.main:app --reload`

Open http://localhost:8000.

## Architecture

```
sahaf/
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py              # FastAPI app, static mount
│   ├── api.py               # REST endpoints
│   ├── classifier.py        # PDF type detection (PyMuPDF)
│   ├── converter.py         # Marker PDF→Markdown + page range extraction
│   ├── epub_converter.py    # EPUB→Markdown (ebooklib + markdownify)
│   ├── splitter.py          # Smart markdown splitting
│   ├── utils.py             # Shared image path rewriting
│   ├── models.py            # Dataclass schemas
│   └── config.py            # Constants and paths
├── frontend/
│   ├── index.html           # Single-page app (bilingual TR/EN)
│   ├── css/style.css        # Lavender dark/light theme
│   └── js/
│       ├── lang.js          # i18n strings (TR/EN)
│       ├── app.js           # State machine + events
│       ├── api.js           # Fetch wrappers
│       └── ui.js            # DOM helpers
├── uploads/                 # Runtime: uploaded files
└── outputs/                 # Runtime: conversion results
```

## Flow

1. POST /api/upload — save PDF/EPUB, return task_id
2. GET /api/classify/{task_id} — PDF analysis or EPUB chapter count
3. POST /api/convert/{task_id}?page_from=N&page_to=M — convert (optional range)
4. GET /api/status/{task_id} — poll every 2 seconds
5. GET /api/result/{task_id} — markdown + image list
6. GET /api/split-preview/{task_id}?parts=N — preview split structure
7. GET /api/download/{task_id}/zip?parts=N — ZIP with embedded images

## API Reference

### POST /api/upload
Upload a PDF or EPUB file. Multipart form with `file` field.
Accepted extensions: .pdf, .epub. Max size: 200MB.

Response: `{"task_id": "abc123def456", "filename": "book.pdf"}`

### GET /api/classify/{task_id}
Classifies PDF as digital, scanned, or mixed. For EPUB, returns chapter_count.

PDF classification rules per page:
- Text >= 50 chars AND image coverage < 80% → digital page
- Otherwise → scanned page
- Result: all digital → "digital", all scanned → "scanned", mix → "mixed"

Response (PDF): `{"task_id": "...", "pdf_type": "digital", "page_count": 270}`
Response (EPUB): `{"task_id": "...", "pdf_type": "digital", "page_count": 0, "chapter_count": 47}`

### POST /api/convert/{task_id}
Starts background conversion. Optional query params:
- `page_from` (int, 1-indexed): first page/chapter to convert (0 = from start)
- `page_to` (int, 1-indexed): last page/chapter to convert (0 = to end)

For PDF: extracts page range with PyMuPDF before passing to Marker.
For EPUB: filters spine chapters by index.

Response: `{"task_id": "...", "status": "converting"}`

### GET /api/status/{task_id}
Poll conversion progress.
Status values: uploaded, classifying, classified, converting, completed, failed

Response: `{"task_id": "...", "status": "converting", "progress": 45, ...}`

### GET /api/result/{task_id}
Get conversion result when completed.

Response: `{"task_id": "...", "markdown": "# Chapter 1\n...", "images": ["img1.png"], ...}`

### GET /api/download/{task_id}
Download single .md file with all images embedded as base64 data URIs.

### GET /api/download/{task_id}/zip?parts=N
Download ZIP file. When parts=1 (default): single .md file. When parts>1: split into N files
(e.g. book_part01.md, book_part02.md, ...) using smart splitting algorithm.
All images embedded as base64 — no separate images folder.

### GET /api/split-preview/{task_id}?parts=N
Preview how the markdown would be split. Returns part sizes and first lines.

Response: `{"total_parts": 5, "total_chars": 361895, "parts": [{"part": 1, "chars": 66474, "lines": 991, "starts_with": "..."}, ...]}`

### GET /api/images/{task_id}/{name}
Serve extracted image by name (used by web preview).

## Smart Splitting Algorithm

The splitter finds optimal cut points in markdown text:
1. Scans for candidate split points: H1 headers (score 100), H2 (90), horizontal rules (85), H3 (80), H4-H6 (70), paragraph breaks (50)
2. Calculates ideal equal-size positions
3. For each ideal position, searches a ±35% window for the highest-scored candidate
4. Ensures minimum distance between cuts to prevent tiny parts
5. Falls back to nearest candidate if no good match in window

## EPUB Conversion Pipeline

Uses ebooklib to parse EPUB structure:
1. Read EPUB ZIP, extract spine order and metadata
2. Extract all images to output directory
3. Convert each HTML chapter to Markdown via markdownify (ATX headings)
4. Join chapters with `---` separators in spine order
5. Apply page_from/page_to chapter filtering if specified

## PDF Conversion Pipeline

Uses Marker library:
- `create_model_dict()` — loads Surya OCR, layout detection, table recognition models
- `PdfConverter` — main conversion class
- `text_from_rendered()` — extracts markdown text and images
- Models loaded once (singleton pattern with threading.Lock)
- For scanned PDFs: `force_ocr=True`
- Page range: PyMuPDF extracts subset into temp PDF before Marker sees it

## Image Embedding

All download endpoints embed images as base64 data URIs directly in the markdown:
- `![alt](images/cover.jpeg)` → `![alt](data:image/jpeg;base64,...)`
- Supported formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, SVG
- Each .md file is fully self-contained — no external dependencies

## Frontend

- Bilingual: Turkish (default) / English, one-click toggle
- Dark theme (default) / Light theme with lavender accent palette
- Inter + JetBrains Mono typography
- Page/chapter range input after classification
- Split parts input with live preview
- Responsive design

## Tech Stack

- FastAPI + Uvicorn (ASGI web server)
- PyMuPDF (PDF analysis, classification, page extraction)
- Marker / marker-pdf (PDF→Markdown, 95.67% accuracy)
- Surya (OCR engine, 90+ languages, embedded in Marker)
- ebooklib (EPUB parsing)
- markdownify (HTML→Markdown)
- Vanilla HTML + CSS + JS (no framework)
- marked.js (client-side Markdown rendering)
